title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Fields, Framing, and Request Smuggling Defense · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for reverse proxies, forwarded fields, framing, and request smuggling defense and demands the smallest safe proxy hop, framing, and parser-consistency contract with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for proxy hop, framing, and parser-consistency contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Strip and regenerate hop-by-hop fields per connection, control Host and authority, handle Connection-nominated fields, and document request and response rewriting.
Require trusted proxies to overwrite client-controlled Forwarded and X-Forwarded values and validate the observed hop count against every reachable topology.
Reject conflicting Content-Length, transfer-coding ambiguity, invalid field syntax, premature EOF, excess body, and front-end or origin disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Proxy Hop By Hop Fields

content.text: A quiz team handles case 7965 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject untrusted topology, ambiguous framing, and insecure parser policy; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Strip and regenerate hop-by-hop fields per connection, control Host and authority, handle Connection-nominated fields, and document request and response rewriting.
